title: Ziegler–Drewnowski–Łuczak–Alon Conjecture (stable Kneser hypergraph chromatic number)
statement_text: The chromatic number of $KG^r(k, n)_{r-\mathrm{stab}}$ is $\left\lceil \dfrac{n - r(k-1)}{r-1} \right\rceil$, the same as the chromatic number of the full Kneser hypergraph $KG^r(k, n)$.
context_text: The hypergraph $KG^r(k,n)_{r-\mathrm{stab}}$ is the induced sub-hypergraph of $KG^r(k,n)$ whose vertices are the $r$-stable $k$-subsets of $[n]$ (any two elements are at distance at least $r$ in the cyclic order on $[n]$). The $r=2$ case was proved by Schrijver; the conjecture asks whether the chromatic number of the $r$-stable subgraph equals that of the full Kneser hypergraph for all $r$. The paper invokes the known power-of-2 case of this conjecture as a key tool in the proof of its main theorem.
paper_contribution: The paper uses the confirmed power-of-2 case of this conjecture (Theorem 2.1, proved in Schrijver [8] and Alon–Drewnowski–Łuczak [2]) as a key step to show that the family of all $k$-subsets of an $n$-set cannot be partitioned into fewer than $\lceil n - \tfrac{r}{r-1}(k-1) \rceil$ $r$-wise intersecting families when $r$ is a power of 2.
notes: PDF source — conjecture stated in prose within the proof section without a labelled theorem environment. Attributed jointly to Ziegler [9] (Invent. Math. 147, 2002) and to Drewnowski, Łuczak, and Alon [2] (Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. 2009). The paper's own original conjecture (the main Theorem 1.1) is now fully proved via Azarpendar–Jafari [4] and is therefore not extracted as an open problem.
